ddls:
CREATE TABLE "media_sources" (
  "media_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each media content */,
  "media_source" TEXT /* Source of the media content (e.g., YouTube, internal recording) */,
  "media_title" TEXT /* Title of the media content */,
  "media_description" TEXT /* Description of the media content */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the media content was uploaded */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the media content */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the media content (e.g., news, podcast) */,
  "keywords" TEXT /* Keywords associated with the media content */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the media content */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the media content was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("media_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transcriptions" (
  "transcription_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transcription */,
  "storage_link" TEXT /* Link to the storage location of the media file */,
  "alternative_no" INTEGER /* Alternative number if multiple transcriptions are available for the same content */,
  "confidence" REAL /* Confidence score of the transcription */,
  "media_id" INTEGER /* ID of the media content being transcribed */,
  "transcribed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who transcribed the content */,
  "transcription_date" TEXT /* Date the transcription was created */,
  "transcription_status" INTEGER /* Status of the transcription (e.g., in progress, completed, reviewed) */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the transcription (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "deadline" TEXT /* Deadline for the transcription */,
  "instructions" TEXT /* Special instructions for the transcription */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transcription_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transcription_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment of a transcription */,
  "transcription_id" INTEGER /* ID of the transcription the segment belongs to */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the segment */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the segment */,
  "transcript_part" INTEGER /* Part number of the segment within the transcription */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text content of the segment */,
  "speaker_id" INTEGER /* ID of the speaker in the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "speakers" (
  "speaker_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each speaker */,
  "speaker_name" TEXT /* Name of the speaker */,
  "speaker_description" TEXT /* Description of the speaker */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("speaker_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., transcriber, editor, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department FOREIGN KEY ("department") REFERENCES user_departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transcription_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history entry */,
  "transcription_id" INTEGER /* ID of the transcription being edited */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date the change was made */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change made (e.g., edit, delete, add) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transcription_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., in progress, completed) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transcription_quality" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality metric */,
  "transcription_id" INTEGER /* ID of the transcription being evaluated */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy score of the transcription */,
  "fluency" REAL /* Fluency score of the transcription */,
  "coherence" REAL /* Coherence score of the transcription */,
  "overall_score" REAL /* Overall quality score of the transcription */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transcription_search" (
  "search_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each search entry */,
  "search_term" TEXT /* Search term used by the user */,
  "search_date" TEXT /* Date the search was performed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the search */,
  "transcription_id" INTEGER /* ID of the transcription found in the search */,
  "relevance_score" REAL /* Relevance score of the search result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("search_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "media_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "media_id" INTEGER /* ID of the media content the tag is associated with */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "tag_description" TEXT /* Description of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "media_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "media_id" INTEGER /* ID of the media content the comment is associated with */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date the comment was made */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "media_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "media_id" INTEGER /* ID of the media content being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the content */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the content was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "department_description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)
